description_long: This object is a stylized biplane with a dark, almost black fuselage and wings, mounted on a circular base featuring a blue and white camouflage pattern. The airplane has two stacked wings on each side, supported by struts, and a pair of thin, protruding elements that resemble machine guns or pitot tubes extending from the upper wing. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a simple, blocky design. The landing gear appears fixed and is integrated into the lower fuselage, with small wheels visible. The cockpit is centrally located and open, with no visible canopy, and the overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military biplanes.
description: A dark, stylized biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and a blue camouflage-patterned base, evoking early military aircraft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and the circular blue camouflage base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the biplane's wing struts, tail section, and the thickness of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the wing structure, tail, and the patterned base.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine (propeller visible at the nose).
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat with no canopy.
